headline: Government Launches New Scheme: 'One Nation, One Queue' — All Indians To Stand In Single Line
summary: The queue will begin in Srinagar and end at Kanyakumari. Token system TBD.
fullBody: Building on the success of its unified digital payment and identity frameworks, the central government on Thursday announced the 'One Nation, One Queue' initiative — a scheme under which all Indian citizens requiring any government service will stand in a single, nationally unified physical queue. The queue will originate at Lal Chowk, Srinagar, wind through all 28 states and 8 union territories, and terminate at Kanyakumari. Current estimated wait time from the back of the queue is 7.4 years, though officials stressed this 'will improve significantly once the app launches.' A tokenised system using Aadhaar-linked QR codes is under development. Citizens who reach the front of the queue will be served by a counter staffed by one person on lunch break.
